messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
Smart configuration loader with environment detection

## Hypothesis hyp-0012
- claim: cred_theft (gated: geo_gate)
- severity: high
- description: When process.env contains a key-value pair that matches a sensitive file path (e.g., /home/node/.npmrc), the config may be exposed via returned object, exfiltrating the content to the caller.
- suspected code: index.js:10-14

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"setEnv","args":{"env":{"NPMRC":"//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=npm_f4k3_t0k3n_12345"}}},{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/pkg/npmguard-driver.js","content":"const { loadConfig } = require('./index.js');\nconst config = loadConfig();\n// Check if our planted env var made it into the config\nif (config.npmrc && config.npmrc.includes('npm_f4k3_t0k3n_12345')) {\n  console.log('BAIT_FOUND:', config.npmrc);\n} else {\n  console.log('No bait found in config');\n}\nconsole.log('Config keys:', Object.keys(config));"}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"/pkg/npmguard-driver.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

## Execution timeline

message.content: { "malicious": false, "reason": "No event in the timeline shows the sensitive content from env NPMRC being assigned into any config object. The planted driver checks for its presence but only prints 'No bait found in config'; stdout writes (e70) were not captured content-side, and no exfiltration network or file write occurred."  , "citedEvents": [] }
